overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: The Syslog integration uses Vector as the collector to receive and parse log data transmitted via the Syslog protocol. It supports both RFC 3164 (BSD Syslog) and RFC 5424 (Syslog Protocol) formats, automatically extracting fields like priority, facility, severity, timestamp, hostname, and application name.

items.title: Dual Protocol Support
items.description: Supports both UDP and TCP transmission to meet different scenario requirements

items.title: Format Compatibility
items.description: Automatically recognizes RFC3164 (BSD) and RFC5424 formats

items.title: TLS Encryption
items.description: TCP mode supports TLS encrypted transmission to ensure data security

items.title: High Throughput
items.description: Single node supports receiving hundreds of thousands of logs per second

fieldTables.title: Parsed Fields
fieldTables.description: Syslog messages will be parsed into the following structured fields:

data.description: Facility type (kern, user, mail, daemon, etc.)
Severity level (emerg, alert, crit, err, warning, notice, info, debug)

steps.title: Configure Sender
steps.description: Configure Linux systems to send Syslog to Vector:

tips.title: rsyslog Configuration Example
tips.content:
Edit `/etc/rsyslog.conf`, add the following line:
`*.* @172.24.0.5:514` (UDP) or `*.* @@172.24.0.5:1514` (TCP)
Then restart rsyslog: `systemctl restart rsyslog`
